title: Quick check: cluster health and audit

questions.prompt: Your Asia-Pacific region's Region Overview shows PBX 80/100 and Extension 1850/2000. The sales team has just signed three customers averaging 60 extensions each. What's the right move?
choices: Onboard them, capacity numbers are conservative.
Decline the customers, the region is full.
Order another PBXHub Server before onboarding; the 80% threshold is the warning sign and three new mid-sized customers will push extension count past 2,000 (the per-PBXHub cap).
Move existing customers to a different region to make room.
questions.explanation: PBXHub Servers cap at 100 PBXs and 2,000 extensions each. At 80/100 PBXs and 1850/2000 extensions, three 60-extension PBXs would exceed the extension cap (1850 + 180 = 2030). Order capacity before the customer signs, not after they cannot be onboarded.

questions.prompt: A customer asks: 'Our PBX had passwordless login disabled this morning, who turned it on?' Where do you look first?
choices: The Operation Log filtered by that PBX, with the edit operation type, in the relevant time window. Edit entries carry an old-value / new-value diff so you'll see the field change and the actor.
The Alarm List for events on that PBX.
questions.explanation: The Operation Log is the audit trail. Edit operations carry diffs (was / is) and the actor identity (Colleague name, Hosting User name, or API client name). Filter to that PBX and the relevant time window; the answer is one row down.
